_about.title: Doctrine-hardening — 7 improvements to the agent/governance doctrine, sliced file-disjoint
_about.serves: task #45 (planning-harden host: gate → fan out file-disjoint slices → negative-control the new code → commit). This is the DESIGN: both planes + a coverage-clean file-disjoint slicing, the canonical-source-vs-reference ownership map, the collision map, and the PREP/reference DAG. The #4/#5 CODE design is returned in the handoff (load-bearing detail), summarized under _code_design here.
_about.domain_choice: technical-architecture — the doctrine is a SYSTEM of artifacts (agent role files · skills · process/ADR docs · governance code); the slices are its modules, the improvements its capabilities. Not the visual `components` domain.
_about.method: decomposition-work, both planes. OUTSIDE-IN = the doctrine artifacts grouped (agent-docs · skills · docs · code) down to the single OWNING FILE per slice. INSIDE-OUT = the 7 improvements decomposed into concrete capability-actions, each split into its CANONICAL-owner action and its REFERENCE actions (repair-the-owner: one fact, one home, pointers elsewhere). The hosts crossing is exactly the ownership map.
_about.slicing_principle: FILE-DISJOINT by OWNING FILE, not by improvement. Improvements collide on files (e.g. #1 and #2 both touch orchestration-lead.md), so a per-improvement fan-out would race. Instead each leaf slice = one file (or one tightly-coupled file group) owned by ONE worker that applies EVERY improvement touching it. That makes the fan-out collision-free in a single tree — which is itself improvement #2's pattern, dog-fooded here.

nodes.label: component-reviewer.md — NC-anchor adversarial check + generator/critic reinforce
orchestration-design — codify disjoint same-tree fan-out + PREP slice, demote worktrees
decomposing-systems — file-disjoint slicing + PREP node · NC-anchor · ADR Decision→action
authoring-components — document the broadened trip-wire + the fleet-sweep/site guards
process.md — gate≠commit + NC-anchor + generator/critic (the gate doctrine, CANONICAL)
#5 broaden the descriptor trip-wire to customStates/slots (component-descriptor.ts compare fns + the static registry the live class adopts)
#4 contract-change fleet migration sweep + /site fail-closed guard

actions.label: pack a SELF-CONTAINED dispatch directive (the worker knows only what is handed) AND name the frozen/committed paths it must not re-edit
Files-changed reports ONLY this slice's paths; never relist already-committed files (report-side honesty)
codify the disjoint same-tree fan-out PATTERN + the PREP slice (land shared scaffolding first); worktrees demoted to the fallback when file-disjointness cannot be guaranteed
the dispatch rule references the same-tree fan-out pattern; worktrees are the fallback, not the default
decomposition slicing guidance: produce FILE-DISJOINT slices + a PREP node for shared scaffolding (the decomposition-side of the pattern)
gate-pass != committed/landed — a green gate is not a landed change (the gate doctrine)
Tests/checks-run states gate-pass is not landing (the gate≠commit clause referenced)
every new gate/probe carries a NEGATIVE-CONTROL ANCHOR — a deliberately-failing case proving the check is non-vacuous
the coverage/probe method requires an NC-anchor when adding a new check (apply the doctrine)
the adversarial critic looks for the missing NC-anchor / vacuous gate, and is structurally separate from the maker (generator/critic reinforced)
reinforce generator/critic separation — a maker never grades its own output; verification is a separate step (the gate doctrine)

fleet contract-change MIGRATION SWEEP: discover every {name}.md + its live class, run schema-validate + descriptor↔props (+ the new states/slots checks) across the whole fleet in ONE gate, so a contract change surfaces every component to migrate
/site GUARD: the site descriptor loader fail-CLOSES on a schema-invalid or drifted descriptor, so the site can never render a broken contract
authoring-components validation loop documents the fleet-sweep + /site guard gates
the live single-source for non-attribute surface: static customStates / static slots registries on the component (mirrors static props), the contract the trip-wire binds to
compareDescriptorToStates / compareDescriptorToSlots: descriptor.customStates/slots in faithful BIJECTION with the static registries (DRIFT_* codes, reusing the attributes pattern)
authoring-components documents the customStates/slots registries + the broadened trip-wire in the descriptor + DoD steps
decomp COMPLETENESS pass: every ratified ADR Decision becomes an INSIDE-OUT action node in the decomposition it drives (no Decision without a hosted action)
the FORESEEN-AMENDMENT ADR pattern: a forward relationship recording an ADR that anticipates a future extension/amendment, distinct from supersede/deprecate (e.g. ADR-0006 <-> ADR-0012)

_ownership_map._rule: repair-the-owner: each FACT has exactly one canonical home; every other touch is a pointer (reference-by-name/ID). A reference slice must point at the owner's settled anchor, never restate the fact.

improvement_1_self_contained_directives.also_canonical_distinct_fact: s2 handoff-contract.md (Files-changed reports only this slice's paths — a distinct, report-side fact, NOT a duplicate)

improvement_2_fan_out_pattern.canonical: s3 orchestration-design (the disjoint same-tree fan-out + PREP slice + worktree-as-fallback pattern)
references: decomposing-systems s4 owns the decomposition-side 'produce file-disjoint slices' guidance — adjacent, not a duplicate
agents/README.md 'Context isolation' invariant should be re-pointed (flagged: README is an implied touch, fold into s1's worker or a tiny s-readme follow-on)

improvement_3_gate_ne_commit_nc_gencriti.canonical: s5 process.md §1 (gate≠commit; NC-anchor for every new gate; generator/critic separation reinforced)
references: s4 decomposing-systems (NC-anchor applied to the coverage/probe method)
s6 component-reviewer.md (adversarial NC-anchor check + critic separate from maker)

improvement_4_migration_sweep_site_guard.canonical: s9 CODE (the fleet sweep + the /site fail-closed guard)
references: s10 authoring-components validation loop documents the gates

improvement_5_broaden_tripwire.canonical: s8 CODE (component-descriptor.ts compare fns + the static customStates/slots registry convention the live class adopts — button.ts)
references: s10 authoring-components documents the registries + broadened trip-wire

improvement_6_adr_decision_to_action.canonical: s4 decomposing-systems (the ADR Decision→action completeness pass)

_collision_map._purpose: files touched by >1 improvement — the reason slicing is by-file, not by-improvement.
implied_touches_flagged: agents/README.md ('Context isolation' invariant names worktrees — should be re-pointed for #2; not in the named file set, so flagged as a follow-on, not silently bundled)

button.ts (#5 needs the static registry adopted for the trip-wire to bind a live source; named scope was component-descriptor.ts + authoring-components, so button.ts adoption is flagged as the required consequence)

_prep_dag._note: the reference slices must point at SETTLED anchors, so canonical-owner slices land first (improvement #2's PREP-slice pattern, applied to this very effort).
then: s9 depends on s8 (the sweep runs the new states/slots checks)
reference_wave: s4 -> {s3 pattern, s5 NC} (also owns a18, self-contained)

_code_design._note: summary; the load-bearing detail is in the handoff to planning-harden.
_code_design.s8_improvement_5: Add `static customStates` / `static slots` (`as const` string arrays) to the component as the single source — the exact pattern `static props` already uses for attributes. component-descriptor.ts gains `compareDescriptorToStates(descStates, liveStates)` + `compareDescriptorToSlots(descSlots, liveSlots)` returning DRIFT_MISSING/DRIFT_EXTRA (name bijection; slots/states have no codec, so no TYPE/DEFAULT/REFLECT). NC-anchor: a drifted state/slot FAILS (mirroring the existing reflect/default negative control). Flag: button.ts must adopt the registries (and SHOULD drive `internals.states.add` off `static customStates` so the registry is load-bearing like finalize, not a second copy).
_code_design.s9_improvement_4: Fleet sweep (components package test): discover controls/*/{name}.md + components/*/{name}.md via node:fs, parse with the canonical parser, run validateComponentDescriptor (schema) on each, and compareDescriptorToProps (+states/slots) for each importable class — ONE gate that goes red when a contract change breaks any component. /site guard (site package): site/lib/frontmatter.ts runs validateComponentDescriptor on load and THROWS on failure (fail-closed, so a broken descriptor can't build the site) + a site test asserting the loaded doc is schema-valid and drift-free. Both reuse the canonical descriptor functions — no forked logic (the drift the process kills).

_coverage_prediction.expected_stdout: == coverage: 15 nodes · 19 actions · 19 hosts == / [OK] coverage clean — both planes cross-check
_coverage_prediction.rationale: 15 nodes (5 non-leaf groups + 10 leaf slices), 19 actions, 19 hosts. Every action is hosted (no UNHOSTED), every hosts edge resolves to a real id (no DANGLING), all ids unique (no DUP-ID), and every leaf slice (s1..s10) hosts >=1 action (no UNJUSTIFIED-LEAF even under --strict). Non-leaf groups carry leaf:false so they are exempt.
